paper_title: Water Quality Assessment by Means of Bio-Indication: A Case Study of Ergene River Using Biological Diatom Index

methods.description: Analysis of physicochemical data to assess water quality in the Ergene River, comparing it to national and international standards.

steps.description: The results of the physicochemical data detected in the Ergene River and some national -international limit values are given in Table 2.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch physicochemical data and limit values from Table 2

steps.description: According to the criteria of the Water Pollution Control Regulation in Turkey, upstream of the Ergene River has Class I -II water quality and middle -downstream of the Ergene River have Class III -IV water quality in general.
steps.searchable_summary: classify water quality based on Turkish Water Pollution Control Regulation

steps.description: In a study performed in the Meriç, Tunca and Ergene Rivers, water qualities were investigated.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze water quality data from Meriç, Tunca, and Ergene Rivers
inputs: Meriç, Tunca, and Ergene Rivers data

methods.description: Collection and analysis of biological data, specifically diatom species, to assess water quality in the Ergene River.

steps.description: During the present investigation, a total of 31 diatom species were identified from the epipelic (EPP) habitat of the Ergene River by counting a total of 497 valves in the upstream samples.
steps.searchable_summary: identify and count diatom species from EPP habitat

steps.description: A list of identified diatom taxa with the frequency values of the investigated stations is given in Table 3.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze frequency of diatom taxa from Table 3

steps.description: The microscopy pictures of identified diatoms are given in Figure 2 and the relative abundance values of the detected EPP diatoms (higher than 1%) are given in Figure 3.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize diatom microscopy images and relative abundance
